fire system: In January 2022, the board approved payment to Cintas Fire Protection for $2,932.77. Since then: alarms have been reinstalled in the dining room and lobby; the property passed fire alarm inspection; City of College Park grant/ARPA grant was approved for replacing the fire suppression system. Most recently, in November 2025, smoke from a resident unit due to a burned pot prompted a Fire Department call. View full timeline →

laundry equipment: In January 2020, resident discussed funds for laundry; written agreement concerning shared laundry fees with $3,454.54 deposited into Resident Council account. Since then: the board discussed the following: cameras to be installed in laundry room as part of security upgrade; multiple machines reported not to be working in the laundry room, converting laundry and dryer machines from coin to card acceptance; residents discussed laundry facility having problems every month and inquired about maintenance; new machines purchased. Most recently, in March 2026, laundry equipment reported as out of service. View full timeline →
outdoor grills: Outdoor Grills was mentioned once, in June 2022: need for two new outdoor grills at Attick Towers, approved spending up to $2,000 from resident fund. View full timeline →
elevator: In April 2020, elevator maintenance invoice approved for $4,225.25. Since then: elevators were repaired; invoice from Thyssenkrupp Elevator Co. for $2,305.29 approved for payment; invoice approved for elevator maintenance contract for $2,419.26; TK Elevators submitted invoices for elevator contract; invoices approved for elevator contract and were paid; elevator replacement discussed; emergency proposal to HUD to provide funds for replacement at Attick Towers; approved multiple other payments for repair and maintenance. Frequent elevator break downs were still recorded, and residents have been stuck in a broken elevator at Attick Towers after. Modernization of both elevators at a cost of $985K were discussed and began; cargo elevator floor was laid, painting of elevator doors discussed, hundreds of thousands of dollars discussed as payment for elevator-related issues. Most recently, in February 2026, a motion was approved to pay TK Elevators invoice for $7,152.87. View full timeline →

hvac system: In November 2020, HVAC upgrades were discussed as part of the budget, water treatment for cooling tower was included in budget discussion. Since then: an invoice approved for Nick DiBattista for property HVAC work at a cost of $15,378.00; problems with the chiller were repaired; outside boiler/HVAC unit was damaged by a fallen tree due to storm damage, covered under insurance with 1% deductible of $15M; HACCP awarded $357K to be utilized for the HVAC/plumbing replacement; HVAC replacement project to be funded using ARPA funds and CDBG grant, approved for Attick Towers; All HVAC units at Attick Towers to be replaced, construction expected to begin first week in March; HVAC installations starting March 25, 2024; HVAC system installed in Mr. Taylor's residence; chiller outage reported, AC work completed; Board has applied for multiple grants for replacements. Most recently, in January 2026, boiler was out of use and questions on boiler replacement were discussed. View full timeline →
